name: Vallespinoso de Aguilar – circular desde Salinas de Pisuerga
region_intro: Touring cycling around Salinas De Pisuerga offers routes through a landscape characterized by river valleys, varied elevations, and rural villages. The region features the Pisuerga River and the Aguilar Reservoir, providing diverse scenery for cyclists. Routes often include climbs and descents, reflecting the area's undulating terrain. This makes for engaging rides suitable for those seeking a mix of natural and cultural exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Salinas De Pisuerga The most popular touring cycling route is Herreruela de Castilleria y San Cebrián de Mudá – circular desde Perapertú, a 18.3 miles (29.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through rural settings. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vallespinoso de Aguilar – circular desde Salinas de Pisuerga, a difficult 16.9 miles (27.2 km) path. This circular route provides views of the surrounding countryside and requires a good level of fitness. Local touring cyclists also love the Hermitage of Santa Eulalia – Aguilar Reservoir loop from Salinas de Pisuerga, a 29.0 miles (46.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain including sections near the Aguilar Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Touring cycling around Salinas De Pisuerga is defined by river valleys, reservoir views, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Salinas De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Salinas De Pisuerga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Salinas De Pisuerga?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes in the Salinas De Pisuerga region. The majority, about 40, are classified as difficult, while there are also 16 moderate and 4 easy routes available.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy touring cycling routes around Salinas De Pisuerga. These routes offer a gentler introduction to the region's landscapes, which are generally characterized by varied elevations.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Salinas De Pisuerga?
A: The terrain around Salinas De Pisuerga is undulating, featuring river valleys, rolling hills, and views of the Aguilar Reservoir. Routes often include both climbs and descents, providing a diverse and engaging cycling experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1174349817/vallespinoso-de-aguilar-circular-desde-salinas-de-pisuerga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallespinoso de Aguilar – circular desde Salinas de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, offering views of the surrounding countryside. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170968316/herreruela-de-castilleria-y-san-cebrian-de-muda-circular-desde-perapertu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herreruela de Castilleria y San Cebrián de Mudá – circular desde Perapertú&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through rural settings.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes often pass by interesting sights. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Pisuerga Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4848732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María la Real Monastery&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Eulalia – Aguilar Reservoir loop from Salinas de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Aguilar Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Eulalia – Aguilar Reservoir loop from Salinas de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is a 46.6 km route that includes sections near the Aguilar Reservoir, providing excellent views of the water and surrounding landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Salinas De Pisuerga?
A: The region experiences a continental climate with warm, dry summers and cold winters. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and less extreme weather, making them ideal for exploring the varied terrain.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera de Pisuerga – Herreruela de Castillería loop from Salinas de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 49.1 km route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170968316/herreruela-de-castilleria-y-san-cebrian-de-muda-circular-desde-perapertu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herreruela de Castilleria y San Cebrián de Mudá – circular desde Perapertú&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 29.5 km with substantial climbing.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Salinas De Pisuerga?
A: The touring cycling routes in Salinas De Pisuerga are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 out of 5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse scenery, from river valleys to reservoir views, and the engaging nature of the undulating terrain.

Q: Where can I find parking for cycling routes in the area?
A: Parking is available at various points around the region. For instance, the highlight &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3306408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Good place to spend the night&lt;/a&gt; near the Aguilar Reservoir is noted as a suitable parking spot. Many routes also start from villages like Salinas de Pisuerga, where local parking options can be found.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through towns or villages where I might find cafes?
A: Routes often connect rural villages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Pisuerga Bridge – Cervera de Pisuerga loop from Salinas de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; passes through &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2076803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt;, a settlement where you are likely to find amenities like cafes for a stop.
tips: Salinas de Pisuerga is a small town where the medieval bridge that crosses the waters of the Pisuerga River deserves special attention. It was built in the 16th century and was converted into the main access road to the municipality.
Nice railway bridge that crosses the Pisuerga river on the outskirts of Salinas de Pisuerga.

The Church of the Assumption, located in Barrio de Santa María, is an outstanding example of the Romanesque in the Palencia Mountain. Built in the 16th century on the remains of a 12th-century Romanesque church, it preserves elements such as the apse and the nave of the Gospel.

The hermitage of Santa Cecilia de Vallespinoso de Aguilar, declared a Historic-Artistic Monument, combines nature, landscape and art. Its construction in the 12th century presents a rectangular nave with a cannon cover and a semicircular apse. Its exterior stands out with well-worked ashlars, a peculiar tower and an imposing cover. The interior impresses with its verticality and a beautiful staircase that joins the nave and head. Sculptures such as the Samson capital and the acanthus leaf capital accentuate its beauty.
Old parish from the 12th century of a medieval town located on the Palencia Mountain.
